source.title: Temu US User Decline, Tariff Impact, and SCOTUS Ruling

extractions.claim: Temu US MAU plummeted from ~34M (May 2024) to <8M (May 2025) — a 76% decline driven by tariffs and de minimis removal
extractions.evidence: Temu's user base in the U.S. plummeted—from nearly 34 million monthly users in May 2024 to fewer than 8 million in May 2025.

verification.verifier_notes: Multiple sources confirm this dramatic decline. App Store ranking dropped from top 10 to #73.

extractions.claim: Temu added 130-150% 'import charges' to US orders after tariffs, effectively doubling prices (e.g., $18.47 dress became $44.68)
extractions.evidence: Temu started adding 'import charges' ranging between 130% and 150%, more than doubling the cost of items. A summer dress sold for $18.47 would cost $44.68 after import charges.

verification.verifier_notes: From CNBC report, April 2025. Prices now matching or exceeding domestic competitors.

extractions.claim: SCOTUS struck down IEEPA tariffs (Feb 20, 2026, 6-3 ruling) but de minimis exemption status remains unclear; cheap import tariffs still in effect as of March 2026
extractions.evidence: Supreme Court ruled 6-3 in Learning Resources, Inc. v. Trump that IEEPA tariffs exceeded presidential authority. However, de minimis exemption status unclear. Trump imposed new 10% tariff under Section 122 of Trade Act of 1974.

verification.verifier_notes: Marketplace.org (March 3, 2026): 'Not much has changed since the Supreme Court ruling when it comes to cheap things ordered from abroad.' New Section 122 tariffs face their own legal challenges.

extractions.claim: Temu pivoted to US domestic fulfillment model with local sellers, marking items 'no import charges' from US warehouses
extractions.evidence: Temu stated 'All sales in the U.S. are now handled by locally based sellers, with orders fulfilled from within the country.' Over 75% of promoted 'lightning deals' flagged as locally shipped.

verification.verifier_notes: Strategic pivot from cross-border to local fulfillment. Key question: can Temu maintain price advantage with US-warehoused goods?

extractions.claim: Temu global MAU reached 416.5M in Q2 2025 (+68% YoY), later surpassing 530M. EU MAU +74% YoY, LatAm +122% YoY, offsetting US decline
extractions.evidence: Q2 2025 global MAU: 416.5M (+68% YoY, +3% QoQ). EU share: 34% (+74% YoY). LatAm: 26% (+122% YoY). US share fell to 11% of global users (-28% YoY).

verification.verifier_notes: US decline offset by explosive EU and LatAm growth. Global trajectory remains strongly positive.
